pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If no non-TS realization exists in-tree, any positive port ROI claim is speculative only.
If the hexa mirror overclaims parity with the TS runtime, the realized-port ROI claim fails.
If migration and default-resolution behavior are unverified, the realized-port ROI claim fails.
If the remaining TS-only surface is thin, low-coupling, and portable, more port work should be promoted now.
If K < 3 or conceptual correlation >= 0.7, the cycle fails raw 48.
If repo evidence proves the legacy migration shim is isolated and dead, it can be removed now without a usage window.
A drill witness must leave a bounded next move; otherwise this round is non-actionable.

axes.name: realization-status
axes.finding: Positive for bounded port ROI. The repo contains a real hexa mirror covering defaults, migration, ordering, file loading, reload, and effective-config synthesis, plus a passing 29-witness hexa harness.

axes.name: scope-boundary
axes.finding: The realized port is intentionally partial. The hexa file states that declaration merging, TUI superclass dispatch, and the full base TUI registry are non-goals.

axes.name: remaining-ts-seams
axes.finding: Further parity work remains negative today. The TS side still owns declaration merging, TuiKeybindingsManager inheritance, global manager installation, and the base TUI dispatch surface.

axes.name: legacy-cleanup-gate
axes.finding: The only obvious reductive follow-up is dropping KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS, but that move is now even less isolated than the previous artifact stated because startup migration code in src/migrations.ts also depends on it.

candidate_options.name: Keep the landed hexa mirror as a bounded positive port
candidate_options.reason: already realized, honestly scoped, and backed by a live 29-witness hexa pass

candidate_options.name: Push keybindings further toward full TS parity in hexa now
candidate_options.reason: remaining work is dominated by TS integration seams rather than portable runtime logic

candidate_options.name: Delete KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS and simplify migration paths
candidate_options.reason: cleanup may be good later, but repo evidence does not prove stale user configs are gone and the migration helper is still used by startup rewrite code

candidate_options.name: Make no further changes now
candidate_options.reason: avoids migration risk, but leaves a bounded evidence-gathering cleanup frontier

falsifier_results.reason: A non-TS realization exists: hive/core/keybindings.hexa (505 LOC) plus hive/core/test_keybindings.hexa.
The hexa file explicitly states its non-goals and does not claim full parity with the TUI registry, declaration merging, or superclass dispatch.
The hexa witness suite passed 29 checks covering platform-conditional defaults, migration winner semantics, order preservation, and missing-file load behavior.
The remaining TS-only surface still includes declaration merging, TuiKeybindingsManager inheritance, global manager installation, and the full TUI base dispatch/registry layer.
K=4 and max conceptual correlation is 0.48 < 0.7.
Repo evidence does not show whether legacy key names still appear in user keybindings.json files, and the migration helper still has two TS call sites: runtime load and startup rewrite.
This round leaves a bounded next move: measure or sample legacy-name hits before deciding whether to remove or retain the shim.

tier_1_promotions.claim: Keybindings port ROI is already partially realized and positive. The hexa mirror is an admissible bounded port of the portable runtime subset.
Additional parity-oriented keybindings port work is still negative ROI now because the remaining surface is mostly TS integration glue, not portable logic.
Dropping KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS is the only clear reductive cleanup frontier, but it remains conditional until legacy usage evidence exists.

roi_verdict.summary: The honest answer remains: keep the bounded hexa mirror, do not spend another cycle chasing full TS parity, and treat migration-shim removal as evidence-gated cleanup rather than immediate simplification.

next_round_handoff.task: Instrument or sample legacy keybinding-name hits over a deprecation window before removing KEYBINDING_NAME_MIGRATIONS.
Do not port declaration merging, global TUI manager installation, or the full TUI base registry into hexa unless a concrete non-TS consumer requires that parity.
If keybindings.hexa gains production consumers beyond the witness harness, re-fire this cycle with consumer evidence and reassess whether more of the TS integration surface deserves extraction.
